title: clematis dioica subsp. acapulcensis
intro: Clematis dioica subsp. acapulcensis, also known as the Mexican clematis or devil's darning needles, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Clematis dioica subsp. acapulcensis include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and alleviating fever, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
